data.title: Brainstorm skips fidelity re-review after significant spec changes including epic splits
data.description:
## Summary

During brainstorm for the Ticket System Boundary Consolidation epic, the fidelity review pipeline ran once on the original single-epic spec. When the spec was subsequently revised significantly — including splitting from 1 epic into 3 epics with restructured SCs, moved scope between epics, and changed dependency chains — the brainstorm skill attempted to proceed to approval without re-running fidelity reviews on the revised specs.

## Expected Behavior

After significant spec changes (epic splits, SC restructuring, scope migration between epics), the fidelity review should re-run on each resulting epic individually. The original review scores are invalidated by structural changes — a right_sized=2 finding that recommended splitting is not resolved just because the split happened; the resulting epics need independent review.

## Actual Behavior

The brainstorm skill was ready to present revised SCs for approval after the split without re-running any fidelity reviewers. The user had to explicitly request re-running reviews.

## Suggested Fix

Add a re-review gate to the brainstorm skill: after any structural change that invalidates prior review scores (epic split, SC count change >2, scope migration between epics), automatically re-run the full fidelity review pipeline on each resulting spec before presenting the approval gate. The approval gate's HARD-GATE should verify that fidelity review timestamps post-date the most recent structural change.
